title: Question (Biedl, Demaine, Duncan, Fleischer and Kobourov)
statement_text: Is it true that $\nu(G) \geq (3n + n_2)/9$ for every subcubic graph $G$?
context_text: Biedl, Demaine, Duncan, Fleischer and Kobourov posed this question after establishing several lower bounds for the matching number of subcubic graphs, including $\nu(G) \geq n_3/2 + n_2/3 + n_1/2 - \ell_2/3$ and $\nu(G) \geq (n-1)/3$. The question asks whether the bound $(3n+n_2)/9$ also holds universally for subcubic graphs.
attributed_to: Biedl, Demaine, Duncan, Fleischer and Kobourov
paper_contribution: Answers the question negatively via Theorem 2 (the characterization $P = L$): the vector $(1/3, 4/9, 1/3)$ is not in $P$ as it violates $x_1 + x_2 + x_3 \leq 1$, and Example 3 in Section 4 provides an explicit connected subcubic counterexample.
notes: The full text is truncated before Section 4; additional open questions posed by the paper authors may be absent from the extracted content.
paper_title: On lower bounds for the matching number of subcubic graphs
